# NagarMitra Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Build and deploy NagarMitra — an agentic, multimodal civic-issue resolver — entirely within Google AI Studio (Gemini API), for the Vibe2Ship hackathon, by 29 Jun 2026 2:00 PM.

**Architecture:** A React + Node.js full-stack app (generated by AI Studio Build) backed by Firebase (Firestore + Storage + Auth). A server-side **orchestrator agent** runs a Gemini function-calling loop over four tools (vision, geo-router, dedup, complaint drafter); a proactive **escalation watchdog** acts on stalled tickets. The frontend streams the agent's reasoning live.

**Tech Stack:** Google AI Studio (build + deploy → Cloud Run); Gemini `3.1-pro-preview` (vision) + `3.5-flash` (orchestration/drafting) + embeddings; Google Maps Platform; Firebase (Firestore/Storage/Auth); React + Node (`@google/genai` SDK).

**Source spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-22-nagarmitra-design.md`

---

## Section 0 — Build methodology: AI Studio vs. "build locally then deploy"

The user asked specifically how Google AI Studio works and how it differs from building a project locally and deploying it. This section is the answer, and it locks the workflow the rest of the plan assumes.

### How Google AI Studio "Build" mode actually works
- You describe the app in natural language ("vibe coding"); an agent (Antigravity) generates a **full-stack app**: **React frontend + Node.js backend runtime**, with **Firebase Firestore + Authentication** auto-provisioned ("Sign in with Google" built in).
- **`GEMINI_API_KEY` is injected server-side automatically** — never in client code. Managed in the **Secrets** panel (add other secrets like the Maps key there too).
- **AI Chips** let you attach capabilities to a prompt: **Google Maps**, image generation (Nano Banana), Google Workspace APIs.
- **Deploy = one click**: top-right **Publish → Cloud Run**. You get a public HTTPS URL; the key stays server-side; it scales to zero.
- **GitHub integration**: AI Studio creates a repo and commits your code; you can keep editing in AI Studio and it auto-commits. **It is one-way** — "We do not currently support pulling remote changes." AI Studio cannot ingest edits you make locally/in GitHub.
- Export options: **ZIP download** or push to GitHub, then run locally with Node.js.

### AI Studio Build vs. local-then-deploy

| | **Build in AI Studio (our primary path)** | **Build locally, deploy yourself** |
|---|---|---|
| Scaffold | Generated in ~2 min (React+Node+Firebase) | You set up Vite/React, Node server, Firebase by hand |
| Gemini key | Auto server-side, zero config | You manage `.env`, a proxy, and secrets |
| Maps/Firebase | Maps AI Chip + Firebase auto-provisioned | You wire SDKs, projects, rules manually |
| Deploy | One-click Publish → Cloud Run | `gcloud run deploy` / Cloud Run source editor |
| Iteration | Prompt + edit in browser editor; auto-commits to GitHub | Full IDE, full git, full test runners |
| Control/debugging | Lower (browser editor, agent-mediated) | Higher (breakpoints, any tooling) |
| **Hackathon fit** | **Satisfies "built + deployed with AI Studio" by construction; fastest** | Risks the mandatory "deployed using Google AI Studio" requirement |

### Decision (binding for this plan)
**Build and deploy in AI Studio Build; use GitHub integration as the submission repo; treat local only as an escape hatch.**

Rationale: the rules require Google AI Studio as the *core build and deploy tool*. The AI Studio path gives us Firebase + Maps + secure Gemini keys + Cloud Run for free, and the one-way GitHub sync means going local is a one-way door (you'd lose AI Studio's Publish flow). So we stay in AI Studio for authoring and deployment.

### How we adapt testing (TDD) to a vibe-coding environment
AI Studio's browser editor doesn't run arbitrary test commands, so we split verification:
- **Pure-logic modules** (`similarity`, `departments` routing, the watchdog predicate, JSON parsers) are **developed test-first locally** in a clone of the GitHub repo AI Studio creates, using **vitest**. Once green, **paste the verified module back into the matching file in AI Studio** (pull-back isn't supported, so this is a manual copy of a small, stable file).
- **UI + agent integration** is verified in the **AI Studio live preview** with explicit acceptance checks (and temporary `console.log` traces shown in the activity panel).

This keeps correctness where it matters (the load-bearing logic) without pretending the whole UI is unit-tested.

### Target file structure (organize AI Studio's output to match)
```
src/
  components/
    ReportScreen.tsx          # hero: capture photo + GPS + note, submit
    AgentActivityPanel.tsx    # live streaming reasoning/tool-call trace
    CivicMap.tsx              # Google Map + severity pins + filters
    Dashboard.tsx             # impact strip (reported/resolved/avg/top wards)
    TicketDetail.tsx          # complaint letter, status timeline, simulate-time
    MyReports.tsx             # user's + co-witnessed tickets
  api.ts                      # frontend → backend calls (incl. streaming)
server/
  gemini.ts                   # GoogleGenAI client (reuses injected GEMINI_API_KEY)
  firestore.ts                # Firestore CRUD helpers for `reports`/`departments`
  agents/
    orchestrator.ts           # function-calling plan→tool→reflect loop
    tools.ts                  # tool declarations + impls (vision, route, dedup, draft)
    watchdog.ts               # escalation scan + draft (proactive)
  data/
    departments.ts            # seeded routing table (Gandhinagar/AMC)
  lib/
    similarity.ts             # cosine + haversine + isDuplicate
    retry.ts                  # withRetry wrapper
tests/                        # local-only vitest harness (mirror of pure modules)
  similarity.test.ts
  departments.test.ts
  watchdog.test.ts
```
AI Studio may name generated files differently; rename/relocate to this structure as you go so each file has one responsibility.

Screens: (1) a Report screen where a user uploads a photo, captures GPS location, and adds an optional note; (2) a Civic Map screen using Google Maps showing reported issues as pins; (3) a Ticket detail screen. Use Firebase Auth anonymous sign-in by default. Keep a clean component structure. Do not add login walls — anyone can report."

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test for ward + department routing (local clone)**
  Clone the GitHub repo locally, run `npm install`, add vitest: `npm i -D vitest`. Create `tests/departments.test.ts`:
```ts
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
import { nearestWard, departmentFor } from "../server/data/departments";

describe("routing table", () => {
  it("maps a Gandhinagar coordinate to the nearest ward", () => {
    const w = nearestWard(23.236, 72.650);
    expect(w.id).toBe("GN-01");
  });
  it("routes a pothole to the Roads dept with a ward-specific officer email", () => {
    const d = departmentFor("pothole", { id: "GN-01", name: "Sector 1-7", lat: 23.236, lng: 72.650 });
    expect(d.name).toMatch(/Roads/);
    expect(d.email).toContain("GN-01".toLowerCase());
    expect(d.officer).toBeTruthy();
  });
  it("falls back to the grievance cell for unknown categories", () => {
    const d = departmentFor("alien_invasion" as any, nearestWard(23.236, 72.650));
    expect(d.name).toMatch(/Grievance/);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run it; verify it fails**
  Run: `npx vitest run tests/departments.test.ts`
  Expected: FAIL ("nearestWard is not a function").

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement `server/data/departments.ts`**
```ts
export type Ward = { id: string; name: string; lat: number; lng: number };
export type Department = { name: string; officer: string; email: string };
export type Category = "pothole" | "streetlight" | "water_leak" | "garbage" | "drainage" | "other";

// Seed wards (Gandhinagar Municipal Corp). Centroids are approximate seed data.
export const WARDS: Ward[] = [
  { id: "GN-01", name: "Sectors 1-7",   lat: 23.236, lng: 72.650 },
  { id: "GN-02", name: "Sectors 8-15",  lat: 23.222, lng: 72.660 },
  { id: "GN-03", name: "Sectors 16-23", lat: 23.205, lng: 72.640 },
  { id: "GN-04", name: "Sectors 24-30", lat: 23.190, lng: 72.630 },
  { id: "GN-05", name: "Kudasan-Sargasan", lat: 23.180, lng: 72.640 },
  { id: "GN-06", name: "Infocity-PDPU",  lat: 23.158, lng: 72.665 },
];

const CATEGORY_DEPT: Record<Category, { name: string; officer: string; mailbox: string }> = {
  pothole:     { name: "Roads & Buildings Dept",     officer: "Executive Engineer (Roads)",      mailbox: "roads" },
  streetlight: { name: "Electrical / Street Lighting", officer: "Assistant Engineer (Electrical)", mailbox: "electrical" },
  water_leak:  { name: "Water Supply Dept",          officer: "Executive Engineer (Water)",      mailbox: "water" },
  garbage:     { name: "Solid Waste Management",      officer: "Sanitary Inspector",              mailbox: "swm" },
  drainage:    { name: "Storm Water Drainage Dept",  officer: "Executive Engineer (Drainage)",   mailbox: "drainage" },
  other:       { name: "Public Grievance Cell",      officer: "Ward Officer",                     mailbox: "grievance" },
};

function haversineMeters(aLat: number, aLng: number, bLat: number, bLng: number): number {
  const R = 6371000, toRad = (d: number) => (d * Math.PI) / 180;
  const dLat = toRad(bLat - aLat), dLng = toRad(bLng - aLng);
  const s = Math.sin(dLat / 2) ** 2 + Math.cos(toRad(aLat)) * Math.cos(toRad(bLat)) * Math.sin(dLng / 2) ** 2;
  return 2 * R * Math.asin(Math.sqrt(s));
}

export function nearestWard(lat: number, lng: number): Ward {
  return WARDS.reduce((best, w) =>
    haversineMeters(lat, lng, w.lat, w.lng) < haversineMeters(lat, lng, best.lat, best.lng) ? w : best
  , WARDS[0]);
}

export function departmentFor(category: Category, ward: Ward): Department {
  const c = CATEGORY_DEPT[category] ?? CATEGORY_DEPT.other;
  return {
    name: c.name,
    officer: `${c.officer}, ${c.name}, ${ward.name} (Ward ${ward.id})`,
    email: `${c.mailbox}.${ward.id.toLowerCase()}@gmc.demo.gov.in`,
  };
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run tests; verify they pass**
  Run: `npx vitest run tests/departments.test.ts`
  Expected: PASS (3 tests). **Paste the final `departments.ts` into AI Studio.**

- [ ] **Step 5: Implement Firestore helpers `server/firestore.ts`**
  Reuse the Firebase app AI Studio initialized. Define the `reports` shape and CRUD:
```ts
import { getFirestore, collection, addDoc, getDocs, doc, updateDoc, query, where } from "firebase/firestore";
const db = getFirestore();

export type Report = {
  id?: string;
  photoUrl: string;
  geo: { lat: number; lng: number; ward: string; wardName: string };
  category: string;
  severity: number;
  aiAnalysis: { description: string; hazards: string[]; confidence: number };
  department: string;
  officer: string;
  complaintHi: string;
  complaintEn: string;
  status: "open" | "acknowledged" | "escalated" | "resolved";
  createdAt: number;        // epoch ms
  lastEscalatedAt: number | null;
  coWitnesses: string[];
  embedding: number[];
  duplicateOf: string | null;
};

export async function createReport(r: Omit<Report, "id">): Promise<string> {
  const ref = await addDoc(collection(db, "reports"), r);
  return ref.id;
}
export async function listOpenReports(): Promise<Report[]> {
  const snap = await getDocs(query(collection(db, "reports"), where("status", "in", ["open", "acknowledged"])));
  return snap.docs.map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...(d.data() as Report) }));
}
export async function listAllReports(): Promise<Report[]> {
  const snap = await getDocs(collection(db, "reports"));
  return snap.docs.map(d => ({ id: d.id, ...(d.data() as Report) }));
}
export async function updateReport(id: string, patch: Partial<Report>): Promise<void> {
  await updateDoc(doc(db, "reports", id), patch as any);
}
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit checkpoint**
  In AI Studio, prompt: "Add server/data/departments.ts and server/firestore.ts with the code I pasted; do not change them otherwise." Confirm auto-commit to GitHub.

## Task 2 — Day 2: Vision analyst tool (multimodal → structured JSON)

**Files:**
- Create: `server/gemini.ts`
- Modify: `server/agents/tools.ts` (add `visionAnalyze`)

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the Gemini client `server/gemini.ts`**
```ts
import { GoogleGenAI } from "@google/genai";
// GEMINI_API_KEY is injected server-side by AI Studio.
export const ai = new GoogleGenAI({ apiKey: process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY });
export const MODELS = {
  vision: "gemini-3.1-pro-preview",
  flash: "gemini-3.5-flash",
  embed: "text-embedding-004", // confirm current embedding model id in AI Studio
};
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Implement `visionAnalyze` in `server/agents/tools.ts`**
```ts
import { ai, MODELS } from "../gemini";

export const visionSchema = {
  type: "object",
  properties: {
    issue_type: { type: "string", enum: ["pothole","streetlight","water_leak","garbage","drainage","other"] },
    description: { type: "string" },
    severity: { type: "integer", minimum: 1, maximum: 5 },
    hazards: { type: "array", items: { type: "string" } },
    confidence: { type: "number", minimum: 0, maximum: 1 },
  },
  required: ["issue_type","description","severity","confidence"],
};

export async function visionAnalyze(imageBase64: string, mimeType: string, note?: string) {
  const res = await ai.models.generateContent({
    model: MODELS.vision,
    contents: [{ role: "user", parts: [
      { inlineData: { mimeType, data: imageBase64 } },
      { text: `You are a municipal civic-issue inspector. Analyze the photo${note ? ` and this citizen note: "${note}"` : ""}. Identify the issue type, a one-line description, severity 1-5 (5 = dangerous/urgent), visible hazards, and your confidence 0-1. Return JSON only.` },
    ]}],
    config: { responseMimeType: "application/json", responseSchema: visionSchema },
  });
  return JSON.parse(res.text) as {
    issue_type: string; description: string; severity: number; hazards?: string[]; confidence: number;
  };
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify in AI Studio preview**
  Temporarily wire the Report screen submit to call `visionAnalyze` and `console.log` the result into the activity panel. Upload a pothole photo.
  Expected: a JSON object with `issue_type: "pothole"`, a severity 1–5, and a confidence value, visible on screen.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit checkpoint** (AI Studio auto-commit).

## Task 4 — Day 3: Geo-router tool

**Files:**
- Modify: `server/agents/tools.ts` (add `geoRoute`)
- Test (local): covered by `tests/departments.test.ts` (routing) — add a geocode wrapper test stub.

- [ ] **Step 1: Implement `geoRoute` in `server/agents/tools.ts`**
```ts
import { nearestWard, departmentFor, Category } from "../data/departments";

export async function geoRoute(lat: number, lng: number, category: string) {
  // Ward from seeded centroids (offline, demo-reliable). Optionally enrich with
  // Google Geocoding for a human-readable address shown in the complaint.
  const ward = nearestWard(lat, lng);
  const dept = departmentFor((category as Category), ward);
  let address = `${ward.name}, Gandhinagar`;
  try {
    const key = process.env.MAPS_API_KEY;
    const r = await fetch(`https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/geocode/json?latlng=${lat},${lng}&key=${key}`);
    const j = await r.json();
    if (j.results?.[0]?.formatted_address) address = j.results[0].formatted_address;
  } catch { /* fall back to ward name */ }
  return { ward: ward.id, wardName: ward.name, address, department: dept.name, officer: dept.officer, email: dept.email };
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Acceptance check (preview)**
  Call `geoRoute(23.236, 72.650, "pothole")` from the report flow.
  Expected: `{ ward: "GN-01", department: /Roads/, officer: non-empty, email: contains "gn-01" }`. (Geocoding may add a real address; if the Maps call fails, it must still return the ward fallback.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Add a "Router" event to the SSE stream** (prompt AI Studio to emit it after vision).

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit checkpoint.**

## Task 5 — Day 3: Orchestrator (function-calling loop) + complaint drafter

**Files:**
- Create: `server/agents/orchestrator.ts`
- Modify: `server/agents/tools.ts` (add `draftComplaint` + function declarations)

- [ ] **Step 1: Implement `draftComplaint` in `server/agents/tools.ts`**
```ts
export async function draftComplaint(input: {
  issueType: string; description: string; severity: number; hazards: string[];
  address: string; department: string; officer: string;
}) {
  const prompt = `Write a formal municipal complaint as JSON {"en": "...", "hi": "..."}.
Addressed to: ${input.officer}.
Issue: ${input.issueType} — ${input.description}. Severity ${input.severity}/5. Hazards: ${(input.hazards||[]).join(", ") || "none noted"}.
Location: ${input.address}.
Tone: polite, firm, citizen-to-authority. Include a request for time-bound action and a reference to public safety. Keep each version under 180 words. "hi" must be natural Hindi (Devanagari), "en" formal English.`;
  const res = await ai.models.generateContent({
    model: MODELS.flash,
    contents: [{ role: "user", parts: [{ text: prompt }] }],
    config: { responseMimeType: "application/json", responseSchema: {
      type: "object", properties: { en: { type: "string" }, hi: { type: "string" } }, required: ["en","hi"],
    }},
  });
  return JSON.parse(res.text) as { en: string; hi: string };
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Implement the orchestrator loop `server/agents/orchestrator.ts`**
```ts
import { ai, MODELS } from "../gemini";
import { visionAnalyze, geoRoute, draftComplaint } from "./tools";
import { dedupCheck } from "./tools"; // added in Task 6; stub returns {duplicate:false} until then

type Emit = (e: { step: string; status: "running" | "done" | "ask"; detail: string; data?: any }) => void;

export async function runPipeline(
  payload: { imageBase64: string; mimeType: string; note?: string; lat: number; lng: number },
  emit: Emit
) {
  emit({ step: "vision", status: "running", detail: "Analyzing the photo…" });
  const v = await visionAnalyze(payload.imageBase64, payload.mimeType, payload.note);

  // Self-correction: low confidence → ask the user instead of guessing.
  if (v.confidence < 0.55) {
    emit({ step: "vision", status: "ask", detail: `I'm only ${Math.round(v.confidence*100)}% sure this is a ${v.issue_type}. Can you confirm the issue type?`, data: v });
    return { needsClarification: true, analysis: v };
  }
  emit({ step: "vision", status: "done", detail: `${v.issue_type} · severity ${v.severity}/5`, data: v });

  emit({ step: "router", status: "running", detail: "Finding the responsible department…" });
  const route = await geoRoute(payload.lat, payload.lng, v.issue_type);
  emit({ step: "router", status: "done", detail: `${route.department} · ${route.wardName}`, data: route });

  emit({ step: "dedup", status: "running", detail: "Checking for duplicate reports nearby…" });
  const dup = await dedupCheck(v.description, payload.lat, payload.lng);
  emit({ step: "dedup", status: "done", detail: dup.duplicate ? "Linked to an existing report (co-witness)" : "New issue", data: dup });

  emit({ step: "drafter", status: "running", detail: "Drafting the formal complaint…" });
  const letter = await draftComplaint({
    issueType: v.issue_type, description: v.description, severity: v.severity,
    hazards: v.hazards || [], address: route.address, department: route.department, officer: route.officer,
  });
  emit({ step: "drafter", status: "done", detail: "Bilingual complaint ready", data: letter });

  return { needsClarification: false, analysis: v, route, dup, letter };
}
```
  Note: this is a *real* orchestration loop with branching (self-correction) and tool results feeding forward. As a stretch, replace the hardcoded sequence with Gemini `functionDeclarations` so the model itself chooses tool order — keep this deterministic version as the reliable fallback for the demo.

- [ ] **Step 3: Define function declarations (for the stretch "model-driven" version) in `tools.ts`**
```ts
export const functionDeclarations = [
  { name: "geoRoute", description: "Route an issue to the responsible municipal department by location and category.",
    parameters: { type: "object", properties: { lat:{type:"number"}, lng:{type:"number"}, category:{type:"string"} }, required:["lat","lng","category"] } },
  { name: "dedupCheck", description: "Check whether a similar issue was already reported nearby.",
    parameters: { type: "object", properties: { description:{type:"string"}, lat:{type:"number"}, lng:{type:"number"} }, required:["description","lat","lng"] } },
  { name: "draftComplaint", description: "Draft a bilingual formal complaint letter.",
    parameters: { type: "object", properties: { issueType:{type:"string"}, description:{type:"string"}, severity:{type:"number"}, address:{type:"string"}, department:{type:"string"}, officer:{type:"string"} }, required:["issueType","description","severity","address","department","officer"] } },
];
```

## Task 6 — Day 4: Duplicate detection (embeddings) + co-witness

**Files:**
- Create: `server/lib/similarity.ts`
- Modify: `server/agents/tools.ts` (real `dedupCheck`)
- Test (local): `tests/similarity.test.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing tests `tests/similarity.test.ts`**
```ts
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
import { cosine, haversineMeters, isDuplicate } from "../server/lib/similarity";

describe("similarity", () => {
  it("cosine of identical vectors is ~1", () => {
    expect(cosine([1,2,3],[1,2,3])).toBeCloseTo(1, 5);
  });
  it("cosine of orthogonal vectors is ~0", () => {
    expect(cosine([1,0],[0,1])).toBeCloseTo(0, 5);
  });
  it("haversine ~0 for same point, >0 otherwise", () => {
    expect(haversineMeters(23.2,72.6,23.2,72.6)).toBeCloseTo(0, 1);
    expect(haversineMeters(23.2,72.6,23.201,72.6)).toBeGreaterThan(50);
  });
  it("isDuplicate requires BOTH high similarity AND proximity", () => {
    expect(isDuplicate(0.9, 30)).toBe(true);
    expect(isDuplicate(0.9, 500)).toBe(false);
    expect(isDuplicate(0.4, 10)).toBe(false);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run; verify fail.** `npx vitest run tests/similarity.test.ts` → FAIL.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement `server/lib/similarity.ts`**
```ts
export function cosine(a: number[], b: number[]): number {
  let dot = 0, na = 0, nb = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < a.length; i++) { dot += a[i]*b[i]; na += a[i]*a[i]; nb += b[i]*b[i]; }
  return dot / (Math.sqrt(na) * Math.sqrt(nb) || 1);
}
export function haversineMeters(aLat: number, aLng: number, bLat: number, bLng: number): number {
  const R = 6371000, toRad = (d: number) => (d * Math.PI) / 180;
  const dLat = toRad(bLat - aLat), dLng = toRad(bLng - aLng);
  const s = Math.sin(dLat/2)**2 + Math.cos(toRad(aLat))*Math.cos(toRad(bLat))*Math.sin(dLng/2)**2;
  return 2 * R * Math.asin(Math.sqrt(s));
}
export function isDuplicate(sim: number, distMeters: number, opts = { simThreshold: 0.82, distThreshold: 60 }): boolean {
  return sim >= opts.simThreshold && distMeters <= opts.distThreshold;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run; verify pass.** `npx vitest run tests/similarity.test.ts` → PASS. Paste into AI Studio.

- [ ] **Step 5: Implement real `dedupCheck` in `tools.ts`**
```ts
import { listOpenReports, updateReport } from "../firestore";
import { cosine, haversineMeters, isDuplicate } from "../lib/similarity";

export async function embed(text: string): Promise<number[]> {
  const r = await ai.models.embedContent({ model: MODELS.embed, contents: text });
  return (r.embeddings?.[0]?.values ?? []) as number[];
}

export async function dedupCheck(description: string, lat: number, lng: number, reporterId = "anon") {
  const vec = await embed(description);
  const open = await listOpenReports();
  for (const r of open) {
    if (!r.embedding?.length) continue;
    const sim = cosine(vec, r.embedding);
    const dist = haversineMeters(lat, lng, r.geo.lat, r.geo.lng);
    if (isDuplicate(sim, dist)) {
      await updateReport(r.id!, { coWitnesses: [...(r.coWitnesses || []), reporterId] });
      return { duplicate: true, existingId: r.id, embedding: vec };
    }
  }
  return { duplicate: false, existingId: null as string | null, embedding: vec };
}
```
  Store the returned `embedding` on new reports so future dedup works.

- [ ] **Step 6: Acceptance check (preview)**
  Submit the same issue twice from nearby coordinates.
  Expected: second submission's Dedup step says "Linked to an existing report (co-witness)" and no second ticket is created.

- [ ] **Step 7: Commit checkpoint.**

## Task 8 — Day 5: Escalation watchdog + simulate-time + ticket detail

**Files:**
- Create: `server/agents/watchdog.ts`
- Modify: `src/components/TicketDetail.tsx`
- Test (local): `tests/watchdog.test.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing test `tests/watchdog.test.ts`**
```ts
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
import { isStalled } from "../server/agents/watchdog";

describe("watchdog", () => {
  const DAY = 86400000;
  it("flags open tickets older than the SLA", () => {
    expect(isStalled({ status: "open", createdAt: Date.now() - 8*DAY, lastEscalatedAt: null } as any, Date.now())).toBe(true);
  });
  it("does not flag fresh tickets", () => {
    expect(isStalled({ status: "open", createdAt: Date.now() - 1*DAY, lastEscalatedAt: null } as any, Date.now())).toBe(false);
  });
  it("does not flag resolved tickets", () => {
    expect(isStalled({ status: "resolved", createdAt: Date.now() - 30*DAY, lastEscalatedAt: null } as any, Date.now())).toBe(false);
  });
  it("does not re-escalate within the cooldown", () => {
    expect(isStalled({ status: "escalated", createdAt: Date.now() - 30*DAY, lastEscalatedAt: Date.now() - 1*DAY } as any, Date.now())).toBe(false);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run; verify fail.** `npx vitest run tests/watchdog.test.ts` → FAIL.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement `server/agents/watchdog.ts`**
```ts
import { ai, MODELS } from "../gemini";
import { listAllReports, updateReport, Report } from "../firestore";

const DAY = 86400000;
export const SLA_DAYS = 7;
export const COOLDOWN_DAYS = 5;

export function isStalled(r: Report, now: number): boolean {
  if (r.status === "resolved") return false;
  const ageMs = now - r.createdAt;
  if (ageMs < SLA_DAYS * DAY) return false;
  if (r.lastEscalatedAt && now - r.lastEscalatedAt < COOLDOWN_DAYS * DAY) return false;
  return true;
}

async function draftEscalation(r: Report): Promise<string> {
  const res = await ai.models.generateContent({
    model: MODELS.flash,
    contents: [{ role: "user", parts: [{ text:
      `Write a firm escalation notice (English) to the next higher authority because this complaint has been unresolved beyond the ${SLA_DAYS}-day SLA. Issue: ${r.category}, severity ${r.severity}/5, ward ${r.geo.wardName}. Reference original complaint to ${r.officer}. Under 150 words.` }]}],
  });
  return res.text;
}

// Pass `now` so the demo "simulate time" button can fast-forward.
export async function runWatchdog(now = Date.now()): Promise<{ escalated: number; notices: { id: string; notice: string }[] }> {
  const all = await listAllReports();
  const notices: { id: string; notice: string }[] = [];
  for (const r of all) {
    if (isStalled(r, now)) {
      const notice = await draftEscalation(r);
      await updateReport(r.id!, { status: "escalated", lastEscalatedAt: now });
      notices.push({ id: r.id!, notice });
    }
  }
  return { escalated: notices.length, notices };
}
```

Add a 'Simulate 7 days passing' button that calls `runWatchdog(Date.now() + 8*86400000)` and shows the generated escalation notice inline with a 'watchdog' activity event."

## Task 10 — Day 6: Hardening, polish, redeploy

**Files:**
- Create: `server/lib/retry.ts`
- Modify: all tool call sites; global styles

- [ ] **Step 1: Add a retry wrapper `server/lib/retry.ts`**
```ts
export async function withRetry<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>, attempts = 3, baseMs = 500): Promise<T> {
  let last: unknown;
  for (let i = 0; i < attempts; i++) {
    try { return await fn(); }
    catch (e) { last = e; await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, baseMs * 2 ** i)); }
  }
  throw last;
}
```
  Wrap every Gemini/Maps/Firestore call in tools/orchestrator/watchdog with `withRetry(...)`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Graceful failures (prompt AI Studio)**
